title: Some behavioral Questions on HR Interviews for Freshers and experienced
author: Priya
content:
Interview guide for beginners


 • what are you most proud of?

~ I am proud of being a citizen of my country who has given us an access of all basic amenities which other countries r not prosperous with. We have good education system , better transport facilities , low and affordable medical aid , free government welfare schemes for poors in food , health and education , good infrastructure development - roads , bridges, housing complex , access to smart technologies , shopping malls , convenience stores and most importantly a low pricing , telecom network facilities for calling and internet usage. And very solemnly I say I am blessed to be born in a country like ours where necessity has become our next affordability for our ease of living.

 • Have you ever mentored anyone who is junior to you. 

~ Many times it has happened that others came to me for knowledge seeking or some piece of advice and I helped them with the most plausible , reasonable response to their query in the way they sought for. But I don't call it my mentorship but just some knowledge transfer from an innovator who is deeply rooted with some knowledge set to pass on to others. And when knowledge is shared it multiplies. So, whenever I helped someone with.some piece of information, I too get enlightened with some new knowledge set vital for my mental upliftment and growth.

• Describe your management skills. 

~ I am very organized person in managing things. I use my art of management to customize things in maintaining a smooth workflow by managing my time and work in a most needed way .Do proper assessment of work which is ongoing , completed or still in the backlog and ensure timely delivery of my work .Give my maximum hours in engaging self with my staff in resolving their queries , in meetings and client interaction to add a finishing touch to my work .

I have many such incidents in my memory which could delineate my work management skills. Once a time I was working on an app development which was to release in production in few days. My team gave its consistent effort in code writing but it was becoming hard to meet the deadline for submission.But than I tried to manage my work effectively to complete it on time. I collaborate with my team helped them in their queries , organized regular meet-ups to share them daily work input and key responsibilities , did periodic app testing after every code integration by team and finally we managed to deliver our work with time.

  • How will you rate yourself on a scale of 1 to 10 .

~ I tried to answer u in the best manner possible but some of my responses were not that satisfactory as it used to be. So, can't rate myself 9 or 10 .But the way I answered u I can rate myself 7 or 8 out of 10. U asked me some questions on threads which I couldn't answer well , some on errors and exception that was I think below to what u were expecting .So, I think 7 is maximum I can rate for myself.


• What's the most creative thing you've ever done?

~ There are many such exciting things I have done for which I got heaps of praises & compliments.And when we do something extraordinary , we get a special feel which ingrained us in working more deeply on it for a succeeding result Few years back , I developed an AI video app that pauses the video when eyes r closed and I got lots many admiration for that by those who used my app. 

And some tiny waste recycle and reuse technique I normally follow at home to build some usable items from household scraps. Sometimes I do some art making by handcrafting waste papers or try some DIY skin remedy from domestic goods.These kind of work may seem normal at once but saves lot of money when we try to dispose off our waste by recreating something that is worth to be valued.

• What's something you wish you had done years earlier?

~ Days of our childhood r the most golden age of our life when we r free from stress of all financial burden , family problems. And I think somewhere I missed those days of my childhood bcoz of poor economic condition of my family and put aside all my fun activities for my studies , job and career.So, somewhere I feel I could have lived up those days of my childhood like other kids.

And 1 more regret I have in my heart of re-appearing in those competitive exams which r age restricted and I failed to get through them when I was in that age .I was good in academics but was too confident with the limited set of knowledge I had and never paid much attention in making myself proficient to crack such exams. And this pride of extreme knowledge and excellence overpowered me and I didn't met with passing score in any of my competitive exams. And it seemed like a lifetime failure for me becoz not able to clear those exams has put me way behind other contending students who fought it and add a good career line for their future.


• What aspects of your work are you most passionate about?

~ From my early days to school working on areas of programming and computing have always been my areas of interest and have taken me too far today in developing good, scalable application , built my understanding on different computing resources and cloud components. And by my continuous hardwork and consistent effort , I have made myself an achiever in working and building dynamic dashboards, interactive softwares , code and commands to simplify my work process and make me more tech ready and divergent to meet current industry demands.

• What words of wisdom do you live by?

~ I believe in my hardwork to get results. If I need a future which is worth seeing , there is no career ride that could give me an yielded outcome without working on it. Hardwork succeed only by working and not by praying to get results. Today, where I am is all with my hardwork and continuous learning that made me skilled to fight for interviews and I am sure if I keep working with the same flow I would get my dream job and a good career growth too in my future endeavours.

• What words of advice would you give your younger self?

~ In my days of playing with baseball , I was loaded with books , pressure of exams and studies . I would want every grown ups to study but not by keeping themselves away from fun activities. Things which I have missed in my childhood I don't want my younger self to deprive themselves of all those fun and play which r a part of our daily happenings. Today, even if I want to spare some time for fun and gossips , I can't do so because growing older is not just adding up of a number each year our responsibility towards our family and close ones increases too .Like u can't eat double of your appetite by keeping yourself hungry for a day , the same way , u can't forgo your happiness thinking If I work today my tomorrow will be happier because in every stage of your life u would find yourself more entangled with daily life complexities.

• What was the most impactful class you took in school?

~ Of my school time all my classes were much informative and engaging too but my keen interest was on my maths class , english and programming . And it was because we apply maths count and reasonings in our daily life , english was my focus becoz I always wanted to be a fluent english speaker and programming was my favourite because with strong fundamentals in programming and logistics I can open many new doors for my career building . This field has wider scope in industry and by keeping my basics strong in programming would make me more Intuitive in working on real time projects . These were some of my all time favourites in my school days.


• What role a dress play in defining our personality ?

~ A dress makes our personality looks smarter .And the way we dress also defines our character.regional identity , our nationality or a disciplinary position at work. If our dress is vulgar, outlandish we could be judged wrongly . Our swanky , fancy outfit garner us with lots of praise and reflects a different image of our persona . Here some among us r dressed in formal outfits just to add a different impression of their self infront of the interviewer . And indeed how we dress plays a key role in describing ourself ,things which they cannot express through words their personality describes with their way of dressing.

• what can contribute to your growth in an organisation -?

~ l work , dedication and determination are the key things attributed to one's growth in an organisation. And not just that having loyalty to your employer is an important factor in winning others' trust on u that accounts for one's career building and organisational growth. If u r doing your work correctly u can place yourself in good eyes of your seniors and colleagues and can put yourself up in the stack in performers' leaderboard. And that 1 step growth can make u reach to an enriching height of your career with your overall growth and development in your company.

• A good position or a high package which is more important for one's growth.

                                Or

If u r given a senior management role but salary is less would u accept that position.

~ Money comes from working , If today I am given a good, reputable position than it would make me more skilled and would give a wide scope for me to earn more and also helps us in excelling in our field of working. And running after money may give us a life of luxury but not of name ,fame or a good image in society. And in actual sense , we don't always work for money but to build a good professional image and an identity of our own among others. And the money earn from our hardwork which may be less but keeps us satisfied because that stash of money will remind us of our hardwork that we invested in our work to earn that money.

• If u have a trainer who is highly experienced and professional but treat his colleagues, juniors badly .Would u tolerate his/her behaviour to work under him..

~ If someone mistreat me than morally or ethically it is wrong and internally a harassment of an employee which is condemnable. And whether u r a highly experienced , well trained employee of your company or a newbie u have no right to treat someone wrongly by any means. And if someone does so it's a punishable act of offence bcoz any bad behaviour against someone could lead to some mental anxiety , stress for an employee and could also ends up in submitting his resignation or termination of his own employment from the company due to such strict , intolerable behaviour of their seniors or other colleagues.

    And coming to me if I have to work under an employee who is rude to me I will keep my work professional and would do what I am asked to do . Because if I answer back to my seniors in a wrong tone than some employee action could be taken on me . But yeah tolerating someone's bad behaviour should also not be accounted for becoz the more I am suppressed , the less will be my exposure to express myself freely that would create an environment of mistrust ,fear and hate among employees with their seniors and others.

• Whom would u offer the job - a poor , unskilled man who needs a job to satisfy their money problem or 1 who is highly experienced if you have to make a fair selection for your company.

~ If I give job to the 1 who is skilled and highly experienced than I won't have to spend a lot in providing him trainings and extra learnings and they can adapt to our working environment pretty quickly. On the contrary offering job to a fresh candidate who has no industry knowledge would take time to adjust in our working environment but since he/she is new, can easily come onboard at a low pay  

My honest selection would be to hire skilled candidate who can contribute in the making of company's finances and in overall growth but still I would hire candidate who is in need of a job due to their poor financial conditions because the 1 who is trained can easily get an another job if I don't hire them but to 1 who has no knowledge of working may not easily get a job anywhere. And by helping him with a job it would be my service towards a poor to help them come off of their impoverished state.

Important HR and Managerial Interview Tips

  • Maintain confidence and speak clearly during HR and managerial rounds.
  • Use practical examples while answering scenario based and behavioral questions.
  • Prepare answers for strengths, weaknesses, leadership and teamwork questions beforehand.
  • Avoid giving overly negative answers about previous companies or managers.
  • Use professional communication and maintain positive body language during interviews.
  • For managerial interviews, focus on leadership, conflict resolution and decision making abilities.
  • While answering behavioral questions, use the STAR method — Situation, Task, Action and Result.
  • Research the company and job role before appearing for HR interviews.
  • Practice self introduction and project explanation multiple times before the interview.
  • Be honest in your answers instead of using memorized responses.
